member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ElasticPool.IsZoneRedundant

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ExtendedDatabaseBlobAuditingPolicy.AuditActionsAndGroups
member.summary: Specifies the Actions-Groups and Actions to audit. The recommended set of action groups to use is the following combination - this will audit all the queries and stored procedures executed against the database, as well as successful and failed logins: BATCH_COMPLETED_GROUP, SUCCESSFUL_DATABASE_AUTHENTICATION_GROUP, FAILED_DATABASE_AUTHENTICATION_GROUP. This above combination is also the set that is configured by default when enabling auditing from the Azure portal. The supported action groups to audit are (note: choose only specific groups that cover your auditing needs. Using unnecessary groups could lead to very large quantities of audit records): APPLICATION_ROLE_CHANGE_PASSWORD_GROUP BACKUP_RESTORE_GROUP DATABASE_LOGOUT_GROUP DATABASE_OBJECT_CHANGE_GROUP DATABASE_OBJECT_OWNERSHIP_CHANGE_GROUP DATABASE_OBJECT_PERMISSION_CHANGE_GROUP DATABASE_OPERATION_GROUP DATABASE_PERMISSION_CHANGE_GROUP DATABASE_PRINCIPAL_CHANGE_GROUP DATABASE_PRINCIPAL_IMPERSONATION_GROUP DATABASE_ROLE_MEMBER_CHANGE_GROUP FAILED_DATABASE_AUTHENTICATION_GROUP SCHEMA_OBJECT_ACCESS_GROUP SCHEMA_OBJECT_CHANGE_GROUP SCHEMA_OBJECT_OWNERSHIP_CHANGE_GROUP SCHEMA_OBJECT_PERMISSION_CHANGE_GROUP SUCCESSFUL_DATABASE_AUTHENTICATION_GROUP USER_CHANGE_PASSWORD_GROUP BATCH_STARTED_GROUP BATCH_COMPLETED_GROUP DBCC_GROUP DATABASE_OWNERSHIP_CHANGE_GROUP DATABASE_CHANGE_GROUP LEDGER_OPERATION_GROUP These are groups that cover all sql statements and stored procedures executed against the database, and should not be used in combination with other groups as this will result in duplicate audit logs. For more information, see [Database-Level Audit Action Groups](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/relational-databases/security/auditing/sql-server-audit-action-groups-and-actions#database-level-audit-action-groups). For Database auditing policy, specific Actions can also be specified (note that Actions cannot be specified for Server auditing policy). The supported actions to audit are: SELECT UPDATE INSERT DELETE EXECUTE RECEIVE REFERENCES The general form for defining an action to be audited is: {action} ON {object} BY {principal} Note that <object> in the above format can refer to an object like a table, view, or stored procedure, or an entire database or schema. For the latter cases, the forms DATABASE::{db_name} and SCHEMA::{schema_name} are used, respectively. For example: SELECT on dbo.myTable by public SELECT on DATABASE::myDatabase by public SELECT on SCHEMA::mySchema by public For more information, see [Database-Level Audit Actions](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/relational-databases/security/auditing/sql-server-audit-action-groups-and-actions#database-level-audit-actions)

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ExtendedDatabaseBlobAuditingPolicy.IsAzureMonitorTargetEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether audit events are sent to Azure Monitor. In order to send the events to Azure Monitor, specify 'State' as 'Enabled' and 'IsAzureMonitorTargetEnabled' as true. When using REST API to configure auditing, Diagnostic Settings with 'SQLSecurityAuditEvents' diagnostic logs category on the database should be also created. Note that for server level audit you should use the 'master' database as {databaseName}. Diagnostic Settings URI format: PUT https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroup}/providers/Microsoft.Sql/servers/{serverName}/databases/{databaseName}/providers/microsoft.insights/diagnosticSettings/{settingsName}?api-version=2017-05-01-preview For more information, see [Diagnostic Settings REST API](https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2033207) or [Diagnostic Settings PowerShell](https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2033043)

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ExtendedDatabaseBlobAuditingPolicy.IsManagedIdentityInUse
member.summary: Specifies whether Managed Identity is used to access blob storage.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ExtendedDatabaseBlobAuditingPolicy.IsStorageSecondaryKeyInUse
member.summary: Specifies whether storageAccountAccessKey value is the storage's secondary key.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ExtendedDatabaseBlobAuditingPolicy.PredicateExpression
member.summary: Specifies condition of where clause when creating an audit.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ExtendedDatabaseBlobAuditingPolicy.QueueDelayMs
member.summary: Specifies the amount of time in milliseconds that can elapse before audit actions are forced to be processed. The default minimum value is 1000 (1 second). The maximum is 2,147,483,647.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ExtendedDatabaseBlobAuditingPolicy.RetentionDays
member.summary: Specifies the number of days to keep in the audit logs in the storage account.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ExtendedDatabaseBlobAuditingPolicy.State
member.summary: Specifies the state of the audit. If state is Enabled, storageEndpoint or isAzureMonitorTargetEnabled are required.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ExtendedDatabaseBlobAuditingPolicy.StorageAccountAccessKey
member.summary: Specifies the identifier key of the auditing storage account. If state is Enabled and storageEndpoint is specified, not specifying the storageAccountAccessKey will use SQL server system-assigned managed identity to access the storage. Prerequisites for using managed identity authentication: 1. Assign SQL Server a system-assigned managed identity in Azure Active Directory (AAD). 2. Grant SQL Server identity access to the storage account by adding 'Storage Blob Data Contributor' RBAC role to the server identity. For more information, see [Auditing to storage using Managed Identity authentication](https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2114355)

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ExtendedDatabaseBlobAuditingPolicy.StorageAccountSubscriptionId
member.summary: Specifies the blob storage subscription Id.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ExtendedDatabaseBlobAuditingPolicy.StorageEndpoint
member.summary: Specifies the blob storage endpoint (e.g. https://MyAccount.blob.core.windows.net). If state is Enabled, storageEndpoint or isAzureMonitorTargetEnabled is required.

member.summary: Gets or sets a reference to the parent SqlDatabase.

member.param: The the Bicep identifier name of the ExtendedDatabaseBlobAuditingPolicy resource. This can be used to refer to the resource in expressions, but is not the Azure name of the resource. This value can contain letters, numbers, and underscores.

member.summary: Specifies whether audit events are sent to Azure Monitor. In order to send the events to Azure Monitor, specify 'State' as 'Enabled' and 'IsAzureMonitorTargetEnabled' as true. When using REST API to configure auditing, Diagnostic Settings with 'SQLSecurityAuditEvents' diagnostic logs category on the database should be also created. Note that for server level audit you should use the 'master' database as {databaseName}. Diagnostic Settings URI format: PUT https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroup}/providers/Microsoft.Sql/servers/{serverName}/databases/{databaseName}/providers/microsoft.insights/diagnosticSettings/{settingsName}?api-version=2017-05-01-preview For more information, see [Diagnostic Settings REST API](https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2033207) or [Diagnostic Settings PowerShell](https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2033043)

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ExtendedServerBlobAuditingPolicy.IsDevopsAuditEnabled
member.summary: Specifies the state of devops audit. If state is Enabled, devops logs will be sent to Azure Monitor. In order to send the events to Azure Monitor, specify 'State' as 'Enabled', 'IsAzureMonitorTargetEnabled' as true and 'IsDevopsAuditEnabled' as true When using REST API to configure auditing, Diagnostic Settings with 'DevOpsOperationsAudit' diagnostic logs category on the master database should also be created. Diagnostic Settings URI format: PUT https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroup}/providers/Microsoft.Sql/servers/{serverName}/databases/master/providers/microsoft.insights/diagnosticSettings/{settingsName}?api-version=2017-05-01-preview For more information, see [Diagnostic Settings REST API](https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2033207) or [Diagnostic Settings PowerShell](https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2033043)

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedDatabase.AllowAutoCompleteRestore
member.summary: Whether to auto complete restore of this managed database.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedDatabase.CreateMode
member.summary: Managed database create mode. PointInTimeRestore: Create a database by restoring a point in time backup of an existing database. SourceDatabaseName, SourceManagedInstanceName and PointInTime must be specified. RestoreExternalBackup: Create a database by restoring from external backup files. Collation, StorageContainerUri and StorageContainerSasToken must be specified. Recovery: Creates a database by restoring a geo-replicated backup. RecoverableDatabaseId must be specified as the recoverable database resource ID to restore. RestoreLongTermRetentionBackup: Create a database by restoring from a long term retention backup (longTermRetentionBackupResourceId required).

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedDatabase.CrossSubscriptionRestorableDroppedDatabaseId
member.summary: The restorable cross-subscription dropped database resource id to restore when creating this database.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedDatabase.CrossSubscriptionSourceDatabaseId
member.summary: The resource identifier of the cross-subscription source database associated with create operation of this database.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedDatabase.CrossSubscriptionTargetManagedInstanceId
member.summary: Target managed instance id used in cross-subscription restore.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedDatabase.IsLedgerOn
member.summary: Whether or not this database is a ledger database, which means all tables in the database are ledger tables. Note: the value of this property cannot be changed after the database has been created.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedDatabase.LastBackupName
member.summary: Last backup file name for restore of this managed database.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedDatabase.LongTermRetentionBackupResourceId
member.summary: The name of the Long Term Retention backup to be used for restore of this managed database.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedDatabase.RecoverableDatabaseId
member.summary: The resource identifier of the recoverable database associated with create operation of this database.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedDatabase.RestorableDroppedDatabaseId
member.summary: The restorable dropped database resource id to restore when creating this database.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedDatabase.RestorePointInTime
member.summary: Conditional. If createMode is PointInTimeRestore, this value is required. Specifies the point in time (ISO8601 format) of the source database that will be restored to create the new database.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedDatabase.SourceDatabaseId
member.summary: The resource identifier of the source database associated with create operation of this database.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedDatabase.StorageContainerIdentity
member.summary: Conditional. If createMode is RestoreExternalBackup, this value is used. Specifies the identity used for storage container authentication. Can be 'SharedAccessSignature' or 'ManagedIdentity'; if not specified 'SharedAccessSignature' is assumed.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedDatabase.StorageContainerSasToken
member.summary: Conditional. If createMode is RestoreExternalBackup and storageContainerIdentity is not ManagedIdentity, this value is required. Specifies the storage container sas token.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedDatabase.StorageContainerUri
member.summary: Conditional. If createMode is RestoreExternalBackup, this value is required. Specifies the uri of the storage container where backups for this restore are stored.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedDatabase.EarliestRestorePoint
member.summary: Earliest restore point in time for point in time restore.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedDatabase.FailoverGroupId
member.summary: Instance Failover Group resource identifier that this managed database belongs to.

member.summary: A blob storage container path to hold the scan results (e.g. https://myStorage.blob.core.windows.net/VaScans/). It is required if server level vulnerability assessment policy doesn't set.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedDatabaseVulnerabilityAssessment.StorageContainerSasKey
member.summary: A shared access signature (SAS Key) that has write access to the blob container specified in 'storageContainerPath' parameter. If 'storageAccountAccessKey' isn't specified, StorageContainerSasKey is required. Applies only if the storage account is not behind a Vnet or a firewall.

member.summary: The administrator login password (required for managed instance creation).

member.summary: The Azure Active Directory administrator of the instance. This can only be used at instance create time. If used for instance update, it will be ignored or it will result in an error. For updates individual APIs will need to be used.

member.summary: The managed instance's authentication metadata lookup mode.

member.summary: Specifies the internal format of instance databases specific to the SQL engine version.

member.summary: Hybrid secondary usage. Possible values are 'Active' (default value) and 'Passive' (customer uses the secondary as Passive DR).

member.summary: The Azure Active Directory identity of the managed instance.

member.summary: Whether or not this is a GPv2 variant of General Purpose edition.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedInstance.IsPublicDataEndpointEnabled
member.summary: Whether or not the public data endpoint is enabled.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedInstance.IsZoneRedundant
member.summary: Whether or not the multi-az is enabled.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedInstance.KeyId
member.summary: A CMK URI of the key to use for encryption.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedInstance.LicenseType
member.summary: The license type. Possible values are 'LicenseIncluded' (regular price inclusive of a new SQL license) and 'BasePrice' (discounted AHB price for bringing your own SQL licenses).

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedInstance.MaintenanceConfigurationId
member.summary: Specifies maintenance configuration id to apply to this managed instance.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedInstance.ManagedDnsZonePartner
member.summary: The resource id of another managed instance whose DNS zone this managed instance will share after creation.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedInstance.ManagedInstanceCreateMode
member.summary: Specifies the mode of database creation. Default: Regular instance creation. Restore: Creates an instance by restoring a set of backups to specific point in time. RestorePointInTime and SourceManagedInstanceId must be specified.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedInstance.PrimaryUserAssignedIdentityId
member.summary: The resource id of a user assigned identity to be used by default.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedInstance.ProxyOverride
member.summary: Connection type used for connecting to the instance.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedInstance.RequestedBackupStorageRedundancy
member.summary: The storage account type to be used to store backups for this instance. The options are Local (LocallyRedundantStorage), Zone (ZoneRedundantStorage), Geo (GeoRedundantStorage) and GeoZone(GeoZoneRedundantStorage).

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedInstance.RestorePointInTime
member.summary: Specifies the point in time (ISO8601 format) of the source database that will be restored to create the new database.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedInstance.Sku
member.summary: Managed instance SKU. Allowed values for sku.name: GP_Gen5, GP_G8IM, GP_G8IH, BC_Gen5, BC_G8IM, BC_G8IH.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedInstance.SourceManagedInstanceId
member.summary: The resource identifier of the source managed instance associated with create operation of this instance.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedInstance.StorageIOps
member.summary: Storage IOps. Minimum value: 300. Maximum value: 80000. Increments of 1 IOps allowed only. Maximum value depends on the selected hardware family and number of vCores.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedInstance.StorageSizeInGB
member.summary: Storage size in GB. Minimum value: 32. Maximum value: 16384. Increments of 32 GB allowed only. Maximum value depends on the selected hardware family and number of vCores.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedInstance.StorageThroughputMBps
member.summary: Storage throughput MBps parameter is not supported in the instance create/update operation.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedInstance.SubnetId
member.summary: Subnet resource ID for the managed instance.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedInstance.TimezoneId
member.summary: Id of the timezone. Allowed values are timezones supported by Windows. Windows keeps details on supported timezones, including the id, in registry under KEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Time Zones. You can get those registry values via SQL Server by querying SELECT name AS timezone_id FROM sys.time_zone_info. List of Ids can also be obtained by executing [System.TimeZoneInfo]::GetSystemTimeZones() in PowerShell. An example of valid timezone id is "Pacific Standard Time" or "W. Europe Standard Time".

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedInstance.VCores
member.summary: The number of vCores. Allowed values: 8, 16, 24, 32, 40, 64, 80.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedInstance.CreateOn
member.summary: Specifies the point in time (ISO8601 format) of the Managed Instance creation.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedInstance.CurrentBackupStorageRedundancy
member.summary: The storage account type used to store backups for this instance. The options are Local (LocallyRedundantStorage), Zone (ZoneRedundantStorage), Geo (GeoRedundantStorage) and GeoZone(GeoZoneRedundantStorage).

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedInstance.DnsZone
member.summary: The Dns Zone that the managed instance is in.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedInstance.FullyQualifiedDomainName
member.summary: The fully qualified domain name of the managed instance.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedInstance.HybridSecondaryUsageDetected
member.summary: Hybrid secondary usage detected. Possible values are 'Active' (customer does not meet the requirements to use the secondary as Passive DR) and 'Passive' (customer meets the requirements to use the secondary as Passive DR).

member.name: T:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.ManagedDatabaseCreateMode
member.summary: Managed database create mode. PointInTimeRestore: Create a database by restoring a point in time backup of an existing database. SourceDatabaseName, SourceManagedInstanceName and PointInTime must be specified. RestoreExternalBackup: Create a database by restoring from external backup files. Collation, StorageContainerUri and StorageContainerSasToken must be specified. Recovery: Creates a database by restoring a geo-replicated backup. RecoverableDatabaseId must be specified as the recoverable database resource ID to restore. RestoreLongTermRetentionBackup: Create a database by restoring from a long term retention backup (longTermRetentionBackupResourceId required).

member.name: T:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabaseCreateMode
member.summary: Specifies the mode of database creation. Default: regular database creation. Copy: creates a database as a copy of an existing database. sourceDatabaseId must be specified as the resource ID of the source database. Secondary: creates a database as a secondary replica of an existing database. sourceDatabaseId must be specified as the resource ID of the existing primary database. PointInTimeRestore: Creates a database by restoring a point in time backup of an existing database. sourceDatabaseId must be specified as the resource ID of the existing database, and restorePointInTime must be specified. Recovery: Creates a database by restoring a geo-replicated backup. sourceDatabaseId must be specified as the recoverable database resource ID to restore. Restore: Creates a database by restoring a backup of a deleted database. sourceDatabaseId must be specified. If sourceDatabaseId is the database's original resource ID, then sourceDatabaseDeletionDate must be specified. Otherwise sourceDatabaseId must be the restorable dropped database resource ID and sourceDatabaseDeletionDate is ignored. restorePointInTime may also be specified to restore from an earlier point in time. RestoreLongTermRetentionBackup: Creates a database by restoring from a long term retention vault. recoveryServicesRecoveryPointResourceId must be specified as the recovery point resource ID. Copy, Secondary, and RestoreLongTermRetentionBackup are not supported for DataWarehouse edition.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.AutoPauseDelay
member.summary: Time in minutes after which database is automatically paused. A value of -1 means that automatic pause is disabled.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.AvailabilityZone
member.summary: Specifies the availability zone the database is pinned to.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.CreateMode
member.summary: Specifies the mode of database creation. Default: regular database creation. Copy: creates a database as a copy of an existing database. sourceDatabaseId must be specified as the resource ID of the source database. Secondary: creates a database as a secondary replica of an existing database. sourceDatabaseId must be specified as the resource ID of the existing primary database. PointInTimeRestore: Creates a database by restoring a point in time backup of an existing database. sourceDatabaseId must be specified as the resource ID of the existing database, and restorePointInTime must be specified. Recovery: Creates a database by restoring a geo-replicated backup. sourceDatabaseId must be specified as the recoverable database resource ID to restore. Restore: Creates a database by restoring a backup of a deleted database. sourceDatabaseId must be specified. If sourceDatabaseId is the database's original resource ID, then sourceDatabaseDeletionDate must be specified. Otherwise sourceDatabaseId must be the restorable dropped database resource ID and sourceDatabaseDeletionDate is ignored. restorePointInTime may also be specified to restore from an earlier point in time. RestoreLongTermRetentionBackup: Creates a database by restoring from a long term retention vault. recoveryServicesRecoveryPointResourceId must be specified as the recovery point resource ID. Copy, Secondary, and RestoreLongTermRetentionBackup are not supported for DataWarehouse edition.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.ElasticPoolId
member.summary: The resource identifier of the elastic pool containing this database.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.EncryptionProtector
member.summary: The azure key vault URI of the database if it's configured with per Database Customer Managed Keys.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.EncryptionProtectorAutoRotation
member.summary: The flag to enable or disable auto rotation of database encryption protector AKV key.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.FederatedClientId
member.summary: The Client id used for cross tenant per database CMK scenario.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.FreeLimitExhaustionBehavior
member.summary: Specifies the behavior when monthly free limits are exhausted for the free database. AutoPause: The database will be auto paused upon exhaustion of free limits for remainder of the month. BillForUsage: The database will continue to be online upon exhaustion of free limits and any overage will be billed.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.HighAvailabilityReplicaCount
member.summary: The number of secondary replicas associated with the Business Critical, Premium, or Hyperscale edition database that are used to provide high availability. Not applicable to a Hyperscale database within an elastic pool.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.Identity
member.summary: The Azure Active Directory identity of the database.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.IsLedgerOn
member.summary: Whether or not this database is a ledger database, which means all tables in the database are ledger tables. Note: the value of this property cannot be changed after the database has been created.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.IsZoneRedundant
member.summary: Whether or not this database is zone redundant, which means the replicas of this database will be spread across multiple availability zones.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.Keys
member.summary: The resource ids of the user assigned identities to use.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.LicenseType
member.summary: The license type to apply for this database. `LicenseIncluded` if you need a license, or `BasePrice` if you have a license and are eligible for the Azure Hybrid Benefit.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.LongTermRetentionBackupResourceId
member.summary: The resource identifier of the long term retention backup associated with create operation of this database.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.MaintenanceConfigurationId
member.summary: Maintenance configuration id assigned to the database. This configuration defines the period when the maintenance updates will occur.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.ManualCutover
member.summary: Whether or not customer controlled manual cutover needs to be done during Update Database operation to Hyperscale tier. This property is only applicable when scaling database from Business Critical/General Purpose/Premium/Standard tier to Hyperscale tier. When manualCutover is specified, the scaling operation will wait for user input to trigger cutover to Hyperscale database. To trigger cutover, please provide 'performCutover' parameter when the Scaling operation is in Waiting state.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.MaxSizeBytes
member.summary: The max size of the database expressed in bytes.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.MinCapacity
member.summary: Minimal capacity that database will always have allocated, if not paused.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.PerformCutover
member.summary: To trigger customer controlled manual cutover during the wait state while Scaling operation is in progress. This property parameter is only applicable for scaling operations that are initiated along with 'manualCutover' parameter. This property is only applicable when scaling database from Business Critical/General Purpose/Premium/Standard tier to Hyperscale tier is already in progress. When performCutover is specified, the scaling operation will trigger cutover and perform role-change to Hyperscale database.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.PreferredEnclaveType
member.summary: Type of enclave requested on the database i.e. Default or VBS enclaves.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.ReadScale
member.summary: The state of read-only routing. If enabled, connections that have application intent set to readonly in their connection string may be routed to a readonly secondary replica in the same region. Not applicable to a Hyperscale database within an elastic pool.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.RecoverableDatabaseId
member.summary: The resource identifier of the recoverable database associated with create operation of this database.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.RecoveryServicesRecoveryPointId
member.summary: The resource identifier of the recovery point associated with create operation of this database.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.RequestedBackupStorageRedundancy
member.summary: The storage account type to be used to store backups for this database.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.RestorableDroppedDatabaseId
member.summary: The resource identifier of the restorable dropped database associated with create operation of this database.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.RestorePointInTime
member.summary: Specifies the point in time (ISO8601 format) of the source database that will be restored to create the new database.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.SampleName
member.summary: The name of the sample schema to apply when creating this database.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.SecondaryType
member.summary: The secondary type of the database if it is a secondary. Valid values are Geo, Named and Standby.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.Sku
member.summary: The database SKU. The list of SKUs may vary by region and support offer. To determine the SKUs (including the SKU name, tier/edition, family, and capacity) that are available to your subscription in an Azure region, use the `Capabilities_ListByLocation` REST API or one of the following commands: ```azurecli az sql db list-editions -l <location> -o table ```` ```powershell Get-AzSqlServerServiceObjective -Location <location> ````

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.SourceDatabaseDeletedOn
member.summary: Specifies the time that the database was deleted.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.SourceDatabaseId
member.summary: The resource identifier of the source database associated with create operation of this database.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.SourceResourceId
member.summary: The resource identifier of the source associated with the create operation of this database. This property is only supported for DataWarehouse edition and allows to restore across subscriptions. When sourceResourceId is specified, sourceDatabaseId, recoverableDatabaseId, restorableDroppedDatabaseId and sourceDatabaseDeletionDate must not be specified and CreateMode must be PointInTimeRestore, Restore or Recover. When createMode is PointInTimeRestore, sourceResourceId must be the resource ID of the existing database or existing sql pool, and restorePointInTime must be specified. When createMode is Restore, sourceResourceId must be the resource ID of restorable dropped database or restorable dropped sql pool. When createMode is Recover, sourceResourceId must be the resource ID of recoverable database or recoverable sql pool. When source subscription belongs to a different tenant than target subscription, “x-ms-authorization-auxiliary” header must contain authentication token for the source tenant. For more details about “x-ms-authorization-auxiliary” header see https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/authenticate-multi-tenant

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.UseFreeLimit
member.summary: Whether or not the database uses free monthly limits. Allowed on one database in a subscription.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.CreatedOn
member.summary: The creation date of the database (ISO8601 format).

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.CurrentBackupStorageRedundancy
member.summary: The storage account type used to store backups for this database.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.CurrentServiceObjectiveName
member.summary: The current service level objective name of the database.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.CurrentSku

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.EarliestRestoreOn
member.summary: This records the earliest start date and time that restore is available for this database (ISO8601 format).

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabase.FailoverGroupId
member.summary: Failover Group resource identifier that this database belongs to.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabaseBlobAuditingPolicy.AuditActionsAndGroups
member.summary: Specifies the Actions-Groups and Actions to audit. The recommended set of action groups to use is the following combination - this will audit all the queries and stored procedures executed against the database, as well as successful and failed logins: BATCH_COMPLETED_GROUP, SUCCESSFUL_DATABASE_AUTHENTICATION_GROUP, FAILED_DATABASE_AUTHENTICATION_GROUP. This above combination is also the set that is configured by default when enabling auditing from the Azure portal. The supported action groups to audit are (note: choose only specific groups that cover your auditing needs. Using unnecessary groups could lead to very large quantities of audit records): APPLICATION_ROLE_CHANGE_PASSWORD_GROUP BACKUP_RESTORE_GROUP DATABASE_LOGOUT_GROUP DATABASE_OBJECT_CHANGE_GROUP DATABASE_OBJECT_OWNERSHIP_CHANGE_GROUP DATABASE_OBJECT_PERMISSION_CHANGE_GROUP DATABASE_OPERATION_GROUP DATABASE_PERMISSION_CHANGE_GROUP DATABASE_PRINCIPAL_CHANGE_GROUP DATABASE_PRINCIPAL_IMPERSONATION_GROUP DATABASE_ROLE_MEMBER_CHANGE_GROUP FAILED_DATABASE_AUTHENTICATION_GROUP SCHEMA_OBJECT_ACCESS_GROUP SCHEMA_OBJECT_CHANGE_GROUP SCHEMA_OBJECT_OWNERSHIP_CHANGE_GROUP SCHEMA_OBJECT_PERMISSION_CHANGE_GROUP SUCCESSFUL_DATABASE_AUTHENTICATION_GROUP USER_CHANGE_PASSWORD_GROUP BATCH_STARTED_GROUP BATCH_COMPLETED_GROUP DBCC_GROUP DATABASE_OWNERSHIP_CHANGE_GROUP DATABASE_CHANGE_GROUP LEDGER_OPERATION_GROUP These are groups that cover all sql statements and stored procedures executed against the database, and should not be used in combination with other groups as this will result in duplicate audit logs. For more information, see [Database-Level Audit Action Groups](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/relational-databases/security/auditing/sql-server-audit-action-groups-and-actions#database-level-audit-action-groups). For Database auditing policy, specific Actions can also be specified (note that Actions cannot be specified for Server auditing policy). The supported actions to audit are: SELECT UPDATE INSERT DELETE EXECUTE RECEIVE REFERENCES The general form for defining an action to be audited is: {action} ON {object} BY {principal} Note that <object> in the above format can refer to an object like a table, view, or stored procedure, or an entire database or schema. For the latter cases, the forms DATABASE::{db_name} and SCHEMA::{schema_name} are used, respectively. For example: SELECT on dbo.myTable by public SELECT on DATABASE::myDatabase by public SELECT on SCHEMA::mySchema by public For more information, see [Database-Level Audit Actions](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/relational-databases/security/auditing/sql-server-audit-action-groups-and-actions#database-level-audit-actions)

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabaseBlobAuditingPolicy.IsAzureMonitorTargetEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether audit events are sent to Azure Monitor. In order to send the events to Azure Monitor, specify 'State' as 'Enabled' and 'IsAzureMonitorTargetEnabled' as true. When using REST API to configure auditing, Diagnostic Settings with 'SQLSecurityAuditEvents' diagnostic logs category on the database should be also created. Note that for server level audit you should use the 'master' database as {databaseName}. Diagnostic Settings URI format: PUT https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroup}/providers/Microsoft.Sql/servers/{serverName}/databases/{databaseName}/providers/microsoft.insights/diagnosticSettings/{settingsName}?api-version=2017-05-01-preview For more information, see [Diagnostic Settings REST API](https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2033207) or [Diagnostic Settings PowerShell](https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2033043)

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabaseBlobAuditingPolicy.IsManagedIdentityInUse
member.summary: Specifies whether Managed Identity is used to access blob storage.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabaseBlobAuditingPolicy.IsStorageSecondaryKeyInUse
member.summary: Specifies whether storageAccountAccessKey value is the storage's secondary key.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabaseBlobAuditingPolicy.QueueDelayMs
member.summary: Specifies the amount of time in milliseconds that can elapse before audit actions are forced to be processed. The default minimum value is 1000 (1 second). The maximum is 2,147,483,647.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabaseBlobAuditingPolicy.RetentionDays
member.summary: Specifies the number of days to keep in the audit logs in the storage account.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabaseBlobAuditingPolicy.State
member.summary: Specifies the state of the audit. If state is Enabled, storageEndpoint or isAzureMonitorTargetEnabled are required.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabaseBlobAuditingPolicy.StorageAccountAccessKey
member.summary: Specifies the identifier key of the auditing storage account. If state is Enabled and storageEndpoint is specified, not specifying the storageAccountAccessKey will use SQL server system-assigned managed identity to access the storage. Prerequisites for using managed identity authentication: 1. Assign SQL Server a system-assigned managed identity in Azure Active Directory (AAD). 2. Grant SQL Server identity access to the storage account by adding 'Storage Blob Data Contributor' RBAC role to the server identity. For more information, see [Auditing to storage using Managed Identity authentication](https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2114355)

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabaseBlobAuditingPolicy.StorageAccountSubscriptionId
member.summary: Specifies the blob storage subscription Id.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabaseBlobAuditingPolicy.StorageEndpoint
member.summary: Specifies the blob storage endpoint (e.g. https://MyAccount.blob.core.windows.net). If state is Enabled, storageEndpoint or isAzureMonitorTargetEnabled is required.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabaseVulnerabilityAssessment.StorageAccountAccessKey
member.summary: Specifies the identifier key of the storage account for vulnerability assessment scan results. If 'StorageContainerSasKey' isn't specified, storageAccountAccessKey is required. Applies only if the storage account is not behind a Vnet or a firewall.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabaseVulnerabilityAssessment.StorageContainerPath
member.summary: A blob storage container path to hold the scan results (e.g. https://myStorage.blob.core.windows.net/VaScans/). It is required if server level vulnerability assessment policy doesn't set.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlDatabaseVulnerabilityAssessment.StorageContainerSasKey
member.summary: A shared access signature (SAS Key) that has write access to the blob container specified in 'storageContainerPath' parameter. If 'storageAccountAccessKey' isn't specified, StorageContainerSasKey is required. Applies only if the storage account is not behind a Vnet or a firewall.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlServerBlobAuditingPolicy.AuditActionsAndGroups
member.summary: Specifies the Actions-Groups and Actions to audit. The recommended set of action groups to use is the following combination - this will audit all the queries and stored procedures executed against the database, as well as successful and failed logins: BATCH_COMPLETED_GROUP, SUCCESSFUL_DATABASE_AUTHENTICATION_GROUP, FAILED_DATABASE_AUTHENTICATION_GROUP. This above combination is also the set that is configured by default when enabling auditing from the Azure portal. The supported action groups to audit are (note: choose only specific groups that cover your auditing needs. Using unnecessary groups could lead to very large quantities of audit records): APPLICATION_ROLE_CHANGE_PASSWORD_GROUP BACKUP_RESTORE_GROUP DATABASE_LOGOUT_GROUP DATABASE_OBJECT_CHANGE_GROUP DATABASE_OBJECT_OWNERSHIP_CHANGE_GROUP DATABASE_OBJECT_PERMISSION_CHANGE_GROUP DATABASE_OPERATION_GROUP DATABASE_PERMISSION_CHANGE_GROUP DATABASE_PRINCIPAL_CHANGE_GROUP DATABASE_PRINCIPAL_IMPERSONATION_GROUP DATABASE_ROLE_MEMBER_CHANGE_GROUP FAILED_DATABASE_AUTHENTICATION_GROUP SCHEMA_OBJECT_ACCESS_GROUP SCHEMA_OBJECT_CHANGE_GROUP SCHEMA_OBJECT_OWNERSHIP_CHANGE_GROUP SCHEMA_OBJECT_PERMISSION_CHANGE_GROUP SUCCESSFUL_DATABASE_AUTHENTICATION_GROUP USER_CHANGE_PASSWORD_GROUP BATCH_STARTED_GROUP BATCH_COMPLETED_GROUP DBCC_GROUP DATABASE_OWNERSHIP_CHANGE_GROUP DATABASE_CHANGE_GROUP LEDGER_OPERATION_GROUP These are groups that cover all sql statements and stored procedures executed against the database, and should not be used in combination with other groups as this will result in duplicate audit logs. For more information, see [Database-Level Audit Action Groups](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/relational-databases/security/auditing/sql-server-audit-action-groups-and-actions#database-level-audit-action-groups). For Database auditing policy, specific Actions can also be specified (note that Actions cannot be specified for Server auditing policy). The supported actions to audit are: SELECT UPDATE INSERT DELETE EXECUTE RECEIVE REFERENCES The general form for defining an action to be audited is: {action} ON {object} BY {principal} Note that <object> in the above format can refer to an object like a table, view, or stored procedure, or an entire database or schema. For the latter cases, the forms DATABASE::{db_name} and SCHEMA::{schema_name} are used, respectively. For example: SELECT on dbo.myTable by public SELECT on DATABASE::myDatabase by public SELECT on SCHEMA::mySchema by public For more information, see [Database-Level Audit Actions](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/relational-databases/security/auditing/sql-server-audit-action-groups-and-actions#database-level-audit-actions)

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlServerBlobAuditingPolicy.IsAzureMonitorTargetEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether audit events are sent to Azure Monitor. In order to send the events to Azure Monitor, specify 'State' as 'Enabled' and 'IsAzureMonitorTargetEnabled' as true. When using REST API to configure auditing, Diagnostic Settings with 'SQLSecurityAuditEvents' diagnostic logs category on the database should be also created. Note that for server level audit you should use the 'master' database as {databaseName}. Diagnostic Settings URI format: PUT https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroup}/providers/Microsoft.Sql/servers/{serverName}/databases/{databaseName}/providers/microsoft.insights/diagnosticSettings/{settingsName}?api-version=2017-05-01-preview For more information, see [Diagnostic Settings REST API](https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2033207) or [Diagnostic Settings PowerShell](https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2033043)

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlServerBlobAuditingPolicy.IsDevopsAuditEnabled
member.summary: Specifies the state of devops audit. If state is Enabled, devops logs will be sent to Azure Monitor. In order to send the events to Azure Monitor, specify 'State' as 'Enabled', 'IsAzureMonitorTargetEnabled' as true and 'IsDevopsAuditEnabled' as true When using REST API to configure auditing, Diagnostic Settings with 'DevOpsOperationsAudit' diagnostic logs category on the master database should also be created. Diagnostic Settings URI format: PUT https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroup}/providers/Microsoft.Sql/servers/{serverName}/databases/master/providers/microsoft.insights/diagnosticSettings/{settingsName}?api-version=2017-05-01-preview For more information, see [Diagnostic Settings REST API](https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2033207) or [Diagnostic Settings PowerShell](https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2033043)

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlServerBlobAuditingPolicy.IsManagedIdentityInUse
member.summary: Specifies whether Managed Identity is used to access blob storage.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlServerBlobAuditingPolicy.IsStorageSecondaryKeyInUse
member.summary: Specifies whether storageAccountAccessKey value is the storage's secondary key.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlServerBlobAuditingPolicy.QueueDelayMs
member.summary: Specifies the amount of time in milliseconds that can elapse before audit actions are forced to be processed. The default minimum value is 1000 (1 second). The maximum is 2,147,483,647.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlServerBlobAuditingPolicy.RetentionDays
member.summary: Specifies the number of days to keep in the audit logs in the storage account.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlServerBlobAuditingPolicy.State
member.summary: Specifies the state of the audit. If state is Enabled, storageEndpoint or isAzureMonitorTargetEnabled are required.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlServerBlobAuditingPolicy.StorageAccountAccessKey
member.summary: Specifies the identifier key of the auditing storage account. If state is Enabled and storageEndpoint is specified, not specifying the storageAccountAccessKey will use SQL server system-assigned managed identity to access the storage. Prerequisites for using managed identity authentication: 1. Assign SQL Server a system-assigned managed identity in Azure Active Directory (AAD). 2. Grant SQL Server identity access to the storage account by adding 'Storage Blob Data Contributor' RBAC role to the server identity. For more information, see [Auditing to storage using Managed Identity authentication](https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2114355)

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlServerBlobAuditingPolicy.StorageAccountSubscriptionId
member.summary: Specifies the blob storage subscription Id.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlServerBlobAuditingPolicy.StorageEndpoint
member.summary: Specifies the blob storage endpoint (e.g. https://MyAccount.blob.core.windows.net). If state is Enabled, storageEndpoint or isAzureMonitorTargetEnabled is required.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlServerKey.Name
member.summary: The name of the server key to be operated on (updated or created). The key name is required to be in the format of 'vault_key_version'. For example, if the keyId is https://YourVaultName.vault.azure.net/keys/YourKeyName/YourKeyVersion, then the server key name should be formatted as: YourVaultName_YourKeyName_YourKeyVersion.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Sql.SqlServerKey.Uri
member.summary: The URI of the server key. If the ServerKeyType is AzureKeyVault, then the URI is required. The AKV URI is required to be in this format: 'https://YourVaultName.vault.azure.net/keys/YourKeyName/YourKeyVersion'.

member.summary: Determines if either an AppContext switch or its corresponding Environment Variable is set
member.param: Name of the AppContext switch.
Name of the Environment variable.
member.returns: If the AppContext switch has been set, returns the value of the switch. If the AppContext switch has not been set, returns the value of the environment variable. False if neither is set.

member.name: T:Azure.Core.AsyncLockWithValue`1
member.summary: Primitive that combines async lock and value cache

member.name: M:Azure.Core.AsyncLockWithValue`1.GetLockOrValueAsync(System.Boolean,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Method that either returns cached value or acquire a lock. If one caller has acquired a lock, other callers will be waiting for the lock to be released. If value is set, lock is released and all waiters get that value. If value isn't set, the next waiter in the queue will get the lock.

member.name: P:Azure.Core.AsyncLockWithValue`1.LockOrValue.HasValue
member.summary: Returns true if lock contains the cached value. Otherwise false.

member.name: P:Azure.Core.AsyncLockWithValue`1.LockOrValue.Value
member.summary: Returns cached value if it was set when lock has been created. Throws exception otherwise.
member.exception: Value isn't set.

member.name: M:Azure.Core.AsyncLockWithValue`1.LockOrValue.SetValue(`0)
member.summary: Set value to the cache and to all the waiters.
member.exception: Value is set already.

member.name: M:Azure.Core.Pipeline.ClientDiagnostics.#ctor(Azure.Core.ClientOptions,System.Nullable{System.Boolean})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The customer provided client options object.

Flag controlling if created by this for client method calls should be suppressed when called by other Azure SDK client methods. It's recommended to set it to true for new clients; use default (null) for backward compatibility reasons, or set it to false to explicitly disable suppression for specific cases. The default value could change in the future, the flag should be only set to false if suppression for the client should never be enabled.

member.name: M:Azure.Core.Pipeline.ClientDiagnostics.#ctor(System.String,System.String,Azure.Core.DiagnosticsOptions,System.Nullable{System.Boolean})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: Namespace of the client class, such as Azure.Storage or Azure.AppConfiguration.
Azure Resource Provider namespace of the Azure service SDK is primarily used for.
The customer provided client diagnostics options.

Flag controlling if created by this for client method calls should be suppressed when called by other Azure SDK client methods. It's recommended to set it to true for new clients, use default (null) for old clients for backward compatibility reasons, or set it to false to explicitly disable suppression for specific cases. The default value could change in the future, the flag should be only set to false if suppression for the client should never be enabled.

When a user instantiates an directly using a public constructor, there's no previous service call. In this case, passing null is expected.

member.name: M:Azure.Core.OperationInternalBase.WaitForCompletionResponseAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Periodically calls until the long-running operation completes. After each service call, a retry-after header may be returned to communicate that there is no reason to poll for status change until the specified time has passed. The maximum of the retry after value and the fallback strategy is then used as the wait interval. Headers supported are: "Retry-After", "retry-after-ms", and "x-ms-retry-after-ms", Usage example:
public async ValueTask<Response<T>> WaitForCompletionAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken) => await _operationInternal.WaitForCompletionAsync(cancellationToken).ConfigureAwait(false);
member.param: A controlling the request lifetime.
member.returns: The last HTTP response received from the server, including the final result of the long-running operation.
member.exception: Thrown if there's been any issues during the connection, or if the operation has completed with failures.

member.name: M:Azure.Core.OperationInternalBase.WaitForCompletionResponseAsync(System.TimeSpan,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Periodically calls until the long-running operation completes. The interval between calls is defined by the parameter , but it can change based on information returned from the server. After each service call, a retry-after header may be returned to communicate that there is no reason to poll for status change until the specified time has passed. In this case, the maximum value between the parameter and the retry-after header is chosen as the wait interval. Headers supported are: "Retry-After", "retry-after-ms", and "x-ms-retry-after-ms". Usage example:
public async ValueTask<Response<T>> WaitForCompletionAsync(TimeSpan pollingInterval, CancellationToken cancellationToken) => await _operationInternal.WaitForCompletionAsync(pollingInterval, cancellationToken).ConfigureAwait(false);
member.param: The interval between status requests to the server.
A controlling the request lifetime.
member.returns: The last HTTP response received from the server, including the final result of the long-running operation.
member.exception: Thrown if there's been any issues during the connection, or if the operation has completed with failures.

member.name: P:Azure.Core.ForwardsClientCallsAttribute.SkipChecks
member.summary: Gets whether or not we should validate DiagnosticScope for this API. In the case where there is an internal API that makes the Azure API call and a public API that uses it we need ForwardsClientCalls. If the public API will cache the results then the diagnostic scope will not always be created because an Azure API is not always called. In this case we need to turn off this validation for this API only.
